articles.title: Cabinet approves Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme 5.0
articles.summary: The Indian Cabinet has approved the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme 5.0. This scheme provides credit guarantees to businesses, particularly MSMEs, to help them access emergency funding. The move aims to support economic recovery and mitigate financial distress caused by crises. The scheme is a continuation of previous versions, expanding its scope to address current economic challenges.

This plan aims to strengthen the financial sustainability of RRBs, which play a key role in rural credit access. The approval comes under the DFS, a central government body overseeing financial services policies. The move is expected to enhance the operational efficiency and viability of RRBs across India.

articles.title: EC Issues Notification for Constitution of New Assembly in Bengal
articles.summary: The Election Commission (EC) has issued a formal notification to the Governor of West Bengal, marking the end of the election process and initiating the formation of a new state assembly. This step is a constitutional requirement that paves the way for the new government to be sworn in. The notification is a procedural but critical part of India's democratic process.

articles.title: Analysis of Presumptive Taxation under Section 58 of Income Tax Act, 2025
articles.summary: The article analyzes a proposed change to India's Income Tax Act, consolidating multiple presumptive taxation provisions into a single section (Section 58) for the year 2025. The framework aims to simplify tax compliance while maintaining key safeguards for taxpayers. The analysis is published by TaxGuru, focusing on the legal and procedural aspects of the proposed tax consolidation.
articles.raw_summary: The issue addresses the consolidation of multiple presumptive taxation provisions into a single section. articles.title: GST Confiscation Proceedings Quashed as Stock Discrepancy Must Be Examined Under Sections 73/74
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court invalidated GST confiscation proceedings initiated against a taxpayer after excess stock was allegedly found during a factory survey. The court ruled that the proceedings under Section 130 were wrongly initiated and that stock discrepancies must first be examined under Sections 73 or 74 of the GST Act. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle stock-related disputes and confiscation cases under GST.
articles.raw_summary: The case involved GST proceedings initiated after alleged excess stock was found during a factory survey. The Court held that Section 130 proceedings were wrongly initiated and invalidated the confiscation-related orders passed by the authorities. The post GST Confiscation Proceedings Quashed as Stock Discrepancy Must Be Examined Under Sections 73/74 appeared first on TaxGuru .

The court determined the order was not time-barred, ensuring its enforceability. This decision impacts tax administration and compliance for businesses and authorities. The ruling clarifies the validity of extended tax assessment periods under GST law.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether a tax order for FY 2019–20 was time-barred. articles.title: GST Law Requires Year-Wise Notices, Bombay HC Strikes Down Multi-Year SCN
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that the Goods and Services Tax (GST) law does not permit combining multiple financial years into a single show cause notice (SCN). The court held that each financial year must be treated as a separate tax period, striking down the practice of multi-year SCNs. This decision impacts how tax authorities issue notices for GST compliance. The ruling emphasizes strict adherence to year-wise notices under GST law.
articles.raw_summary: The case addressed whether multiple financial years can be combined in a single show cause notice under GST law. The Court held such consolidation impermissible, emphasizing that each financial year is a separate tax period. The post GST Law Requires Year-Wise Notices, Bombay HC Strikes Down Multi-Year SCN appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Companies Act 1956 | Entry In Register Of 'Members' Not Necessary To Maintain Plea Against Oppression/Mismanagement: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a stakeholder can maintain a petition against oppression and mismanagement under Sections 397 and 398 of the Companies Act, 1956, even without being formally listed as a member in a company's register. The Court held that entry in the register of members is not the sole criterion for establishing membership for legal proceedings. This decision clarifies the rights of stakeholders in corporate disputes and strengthens legal recourse against mismanagement.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court recently upheld a stakeholder's locus to maintain a petition before the Company Law Board alleging oppression and mismanagement, holding that entry in the register of "members" is not the only mode of acquiring membership. articles.title: Political Donation Reassessment Notice Quashed as Issued by Wrong Authority
articles.summary: A tribunal invalidated a tax reassessment notice under Section 148 because it was issued by an officer lacking jurisdiction. The ruling relied on CBDT instructions that set monetary limits for such notices. This highlights the strict adherence required to jurisdictional norms in tax administration. The decision underscores the importance of proper authority in issuing tax notices.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that notice under Section 148 was invalid as it was issued by an officer lacking jurisdiction. It relied on CBDT Instruction prescribing monetary limits. articles.title: Legal Analysis of Applicability of GST on Bar Associations
articles.summary: A legal analysis explains how India's GST law treats bar associations and their members as separate taxable entities, making internal transactions such as membership fees subject to GST. This ruling overrides the principle of mutuality, where associations and members were previously considered one unit for tax purposes. The decision has significant implications for how professional associations manage their tax compliance and financial operations under India's indirect tax regime.
articles.raw_summary: This case explains how GST law overrides mutuality by treating associations and members as distinct taxable persons. It clarifies that internal transactions like membership fees now qualify as taxable supply. The post Legal Analysis of Applicability of GST on Bar Associations appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: TP Adjustments Reworked – ITAT Strikes Down Wrong Comparables, Demands Parity & Scientific Analysis
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has reworked Transfer Pricing (TP) adjustments, striking down incorrect comparables and demanding scientific analysis. The ruling clarifies that comparables must meet uniform filters, FAR analysis takes precedence over search matrices, ESOP adjustments require parity, working capital needs precise computation, and delayed receivables should attract LIBOR-based interest. This decision directly impacts tax compliance and dispute resolution in India.
articles.raw_summary: TP ruling clarifies that comparables must meet uniform filters, FAR analysis prevails over search matrix, ESOP adjustments require parity, working capital needs scientific computation, and delayed receivables attract LIBOR-based interest. The post TP Adjustments Reworked – ITAT Strikes Down Wrong Comparables, Demands Parity   Scientific Analysis appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Cabinet clears amendment to make insult to Vande Mataram a punishable offence
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved an amendment to make insulting India's national song, Vande Mataram, a punishable offense. The Ministry of Home Affairs instructed states and government bodies to ensure all six stanzas of Vande Mataram are sung or played at official events. This move aims to enforce respect for national symbols but raises concerns about potential curbs on free speech.
articles.raw_summary: On February 6, the Union Ministry of Home Affairs in a set of instructions to States and other government bodies said that all the six stanzas of Vande Mataram, that comes to a little over three minutes long, should be sung or played at official events

articles.title: UDF’s near three-fourths majority marked a historic victory for the Congress-led coalition
articles.summary: The Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) secured a historic near three-fourths majority in an election, with the Communist Party of India (Marxist)-led Left Democratic Front (LDF) recording its lowest vote share since 1982. The Congress won 63 seats and the Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) won 22 seats, driving the UDF's surge. The results reflect significant regional shifts in voter preference. This political outcome may influence future government policies and legislative priorities.
articles.raw_summary: Haemorrhaging vote shares across regions, the CPI(M)-led LDF registers its lowest vote share tally since 1982; Congress and IUML led the UDF surge with the former winning 63 seats, and the latter securing 22.

articles.title: CIRP initiation for recovery amounts to misuse of Insolvency Framework: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that initiating the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) solely for debt recovery amounts to misuse of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The court clarified that the IBC cannot be used as a substitute for executing civil court decrees. This decision impacts how creditors may approach insolvency proceedings for recovery purposes.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that the IBC cannot be used as a substitute for execution of a civil court decree. It ruled that initiating CIRP for recovery purposes amounts to misuse of the insolvency framework. The post CIRP initiation for recovery amounts to misuse of Insolvency Framework: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Who will Vijay ally with to form TN govt? Venugopal says TVK chief has ‘reached out to Congress’
articles.summary: TVK leader Venugopal stated that the party's chief has reached out to the Congress party amid discussions about forming the Tamil Nadu government. With TVK holding 108 seats in the 234-member assembly, Vijay needs 118 seats to form the government. An alliance with Congress, Left parties, and VCK would give them 121 seats, potentially securing a majority.
articles.raw_summary: TVK has 108 seats in 234-member Tamil Nadu assembly, Vijay needs 118 to form govt. If he picks Congress, Left parties and VCK, the alliance will have 121 seats.

articles.title: Puducherry election results: NDA retains power
articles.summary: The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) retained power in the Puducherry election. The Opposition alliance, led by the Congress and Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam, won six seats. The results reflect the political landscape in the union territory, which may impact future governance and policy decisions.
articles.raw_summary: The Opposition alliance, mainly comprising the Congress and the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam, won six seats.

The announcement was made by the Press Information Bureau (PIB).

articles.title: How ‘bulldozer justice’ undermines the law
articles.summary: The article examines the practice of 'bulldozer justice'—swift demolitions of properties—by authorities, which creates an image of decisive leadership but also raises concerns about the erosion of legal protections. It argues that such actions normalize the idea that executive authority can override judicial processes, undermining the rule of law. The piece critiques the normalization of extra-legal measures in governance, emphasizing the need for adherence to legal safeguards.
articles.raw_summary: The image of swift destruction creates an impression of decisive leadership, but it also normalises the idea that executive authority can override legal safeguards

articles.summary: The Income-tax Department is intensifying scrutiny of charitable institutions that operate with high surplus margins, premium pricing, and commercial-style operations. The development highlights concerns over misuse of charitable status for commercial gains.

articles.title: The Iran Precedent and the Legal Jeopardy of Nuclear Governance
articles.summary: The article examines the legal challenges and precedents set by Iran's nuclear program governance, analyzing how these could impact global nuclear policy frameworks. It explores the implications for international legal norms and non-proliferation regimes, with potential indirect consequences for India's foreign policy and nuclear diplomacy. The analysis is based on Iran's historical and current nuclear governance issues.

articles.title: Miscellaneous Application Rejected as Tribunal Lacks Power to Review Its Own Order
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) dismissed a revenue application, ruling it lacked the power to review its own order. The tribunal clarified that challenges to evidence appreciation do not qualify as rectification under Section 254(2), which only allows correction of apparent errors. This decision impacts how tax disputes are handled and sets a precedent for tribunal jurisdiction.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that challenges to appreciation of evidence amount to review, not rectification. It ruled that Section 254(2) permits only correction of apparent errors, leading to dismissal of the Revenue’s application. The post Miscellaneous Application Rejected as Tribunal Lacks Power to Review Its Own Order appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: No Section 68 Addition Where Loan Repaid Through Banking Channels & Supported by Evidence
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that no tax addition under Section 68 can be made if loans to related parties are repaid through banking channels and supported by evidence. The decision emphasized that interest cannot be disallowed when advances are made for business purposes using own funds, upholding the principle of commercial expediency. The ruling impacts how businesses document inter-corporate loans for tax compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that interest cannot be disallowed where advances to related parties are made for business purposes and out of own funds, emphasizing the principle of commercial expediency. The post No Section 68 Addition Where Loan Repaid Through Banking Channels   Supported by Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CJI declares Sikkim to be first paperless state judiciary in country
articles.summary: The Chief Justice of India (CJI) declared Sikkim's judiciary as the first paperless state judiciary in the country. This initiative eliminates physical paperwork in courts, marking a significant step toward digital transformation in India's legal system. The move is expected to improve efficiency and accessibility in judicial processes. The CJI highlighted this as a pioneering effort for other states to emulate.

articles.title: Section 263 Invalid as AO Conducted Proper Inquiry & Took Plausible View: ITAT Surat
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Surat ruled that Section 263 of the Income Tax Act cannot be used to override the assessment officer's (AO) independent decision if it was based on a proper inquiry and a plausible view. The tribunal emphasized that revisionary powers under Section 263 do not allow the Principal Commissioner of Income Tax (Pr. CIT) to substitute their judgment for that of the AO. This decision reinforces the autonomy of tax assessment processes.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that revisionary powers cannot be used to substitute the AO’s view with that of the Pr. CIT. It emphasized that such substitution is beyond Section 263. The decision protects independent assessment decisions. The post Section 263 Invalid as AO Conducted Proper Inquiry   Took Plausible View: ITAT Surat appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The US Supreme Court, with a conservative majority, ruled that Louisiana violated the law by creating a second Black-majority voting district. The decision is expected to reduce voting rights for minorities in the US. This ruling has implications for democratic processes and minority protections globally, including potential discussions on electoral fairness and civil liberties.

articles.title: Allahabad HC Quashed GST Penalty as Wrong Provision Invoked for Excess Stock During Survey
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court set aside a GST penalty imposed on a taxpayer for excess stock found during a survey. The court ruled that such cases should be handled under Sections 73/74 of the GST law, not Section 130, making the penalty order unsustainable. This judgment clarifies the correct legal framework for addressing excess stock issues during surveys, impacting future tax enforcement actions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether Section 130 proceedings were valid when excess stock was found during a survey. It held that such cases must be dealt with under Sections 73/74, rendering the penalty order unsustainable. The post Allahabad HC Quashed GST Penalty as Wrong Provision Invoked for Excess Stock During Survey appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Assembly poll 2026: CPI(M) commences post-poll debacle stock-taking exercise in Kerala as it realises there is no magic bullet
articles.summary: The CPI(M) party in Kerala has begun an internal review following its poor performance in the recent assembly elections. The party is examining its campaign strategy, organizational structure, and candidate selection processes, questioning whether overconfidence in retaining power led to complacency. The analysis aims to address the party's significant losses, particularly in its traditional strongholds.
articles.raw_summary: The debilitating rout, especially in the party’s strongholds, has thrown up some big questions regarding the party’s campaign strategy, organisational cohesiveness, candidate finalisation, and efficacy of its political operations, including whether “misplaced confidence” of an “inevitable” third consecutive term in power had imbued the party’s electioneering with complacency from the booth level and upwards

articles.title: GSTAT Appeals Filing Procedure and Compliances at a Glance
articles.summary: The article explains the process for filing appeals before the Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) in India. It outlines key requirements such as a three-month limitation period for filing appeals and mandatory pre-deposit payments. Compliance with these procedural rules is essential for the admissibility of appeals and to automatically stay recovery proceedings. The guidance is particularly relevant for taxpayers and legal practitioners dealing with GST disputes.

articles.title: Interest Paid = Deductible When Obligation Exists – ITAT Allows Claim on Capital Bond Arrangement
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that interest paid under a capital bond agreement is deductible if a clear contractual obligation exists. The tribunal held that since the corresponding interest income was taxed, disallowing the deduction would amount to one-sided taxation, which is impermissible. This decision impacts how companies can claim tax deductions on interest payments in similar arrangements.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT allows deduction of interest paid under capital bond agreement, holding a clear contractual obligation existed; since corresponding interest income was taxed, matching liability must be allowed-disallowance deleted as one-sided taxation is impermissible. It highlights how the Chinese government frames opting out of economic struggle as a public offense, reflecting broader authoritarian policies on dissent and labor. The piece underscores the state's prioritization of productivity over individual freedoms, raising questions about governance and civil liberties in such systems.
articles.raw_summary: It's not about the economy at all: In a system that defines not struggling as a crime, opting out is a public offense.

articles.title: Section 271(1)(c) Penalty Cannot Be Imposed for Valuation Differences Without Proof of Inaccuracy: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that penalties under Section 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act cannot be imposed solely due to differences in property valuation unless there is proof of deliberate inaccuracy. The court clarified that mere estimation disputes do not justify penalties, emphasizing that proof of intentional misrepresentation is required. This ruling impacts tax administration and enforcement practices in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that mere differences in property valuation do not amount to furnishing inaccurate particulars. Penalty under Section 271(1)(c) requires proof of deliberate inaccuracy. The ruling clarifies that estimation disputes alone cannot justify penalty. The post Section 271(1)(c) Penalty Cannot Be Imposed for Valuation Differences Without Proof of Inaccuracy: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 54 Exemption Allowed as Possession Date Considered Over Registration Date
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that for under-construction properties, the date of possession—not the registration date—is the key factor for claiming Section 54 tax exemption on capital gains. This decision clarifies the timing criteria for taxpayers seeking exemptions, ensuring consistency in how the law is applied. The ruling impacts individuals and businesses involved in real estate transactions and tax planning.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that for under-construction properties, the date of possession is the relevant factor for Section 54 exemption. It rejected the reliance on registration date alone. The ruling clarifies timing criteria for capital gains exemption. The post Section 54 Exemption Allowed as Possession Date Considered Over Registration Date appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GST on Hosting Services: OIDAR Classification, RCM Liability & Latest Judgments
articles.summary: This article explains how foreign digital services like hosting are classified under OIDAR (Online Information and Database Access or Retrieval) and become taxable imports under India's GST regime. It highlights compliance requirements through the Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM) and the eligibility of Input Tax Credit (ITC) for registered businesses. The discussion also covers recent judicial rulings that clarify these tax obligations for digital service providers operating in India.
articles.raw_summary: This case explains how foreign digital services like hosting fall under OIDAR and become taxable imports under GST. articles.title: Gujarat HC Dismisses Appeal as Only Profit Element of Bogus Purchases Taxable
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court dismissed an appeal stating that only the profit element (income component) of alleged bogus purchases can be taxed, not the entire transaction. The court upheld a tribunal's decision to limit the tax addition to 6%. This ruling reinforces restrictions on full disallowance of such transactions, impacting tax assessment practices in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that only the income component of alleged bogus purchases can be taxed, not the entire transaction. It upheld the Tribunal’s restriction of addition to 6%. The ruling reinforces limits on full disallowance. The post Gujarat HC Dismisses Appeal as Only Profit Element of Bogus Purchases Taxable appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GST TDS Compliance for April 2026: Due Date, Rules & Key Requirements
articles.summary: The article explains the Goods and Services Tax (GST) Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) compliance rules for April 2026. Deductors must file GSTR-7 by May 10, 2026, and issue TDS certificates to suppliers under Section 51 and Rule 66 of the GST framework. This ensures proper tax deduction and reporting for transactions involving government entities.
articles.raw_summary: This guide explains the legal requirements for GST TDS under Section 51 and Rule 66, including rates, thresholds, and applicability. It highlights that deductors must file GSTR-7 by 10th May 2026 and issue TDS certificates to suppliers. articles.title: ITAT Remands Addition Due to Failure to Verify GST Turnover Instead of Bank Credits
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) remanded a case back to the assessing officer, directing that income should be estimated based on verified GST and VAT turnover rather than relying solely on bank credits. The tribunal ruled that the original assessment failed to verify the taxpayer's turnover through official records, emphasizing the need for proper documentation. This decision underscores the importance of adhering to established tax verification procedures in income assessment.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved estimation of income based solely on bank credits without supporting verification. The Tribunal remanded the case, directing assessment based on GST and VAT turnover. articles.title: Iran remains defiant after U.S. launches new effort to reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The U.S. has launched new operations to ensure safe passage for commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions with Iran. Top U.S. officials stated the ceasefire with Iran remains in place despite recent exchanges of fire. However, few ships are willing to navigate the strait due to Iranian threats. The situation highlights risks to global oil supply chains, which could impact India's energy imports.
articles.raw_summary: Top U.S. officials said Tuesday that the ceasefire with Iran has not ended, despite exchanges of fire in the Strait of Hormuz. Both Rubio and Hegseth said the U.S. was in a new phase of operations designed to ensure safe passage for commercial vessels stuck in the Persian Gulf. But so far, very few ships appear to want to run the Iranian gauntlet in the strait. Nick Schifrin reports.

articles.title: Bombay HC Set Aside GST Refund Rejection for Not Issuing SCN
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court set aside the rejection of a GST refund because the tax authorities failed to issue a show-cause notice (SCN) before denying the refund, as required under Rule 92(3). The Court ruled that the absence of notice and hearing makes the rejection invalid and ordered the authorities to reconsider the refund application afresh. The case highlights the importance of following due process in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: The case involved denial of GST refund without following mandatory procedure under Rule 92(3). The Court held that absence of notice and hearing invalidates the rejection and ordered fresh consideration. The post Bombay HC Set Aside GST Refund Rejection for Not Issuing SCN appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Allahabad HC Quashed ITC Blocking Order for Failure to Record Reason to Believe
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court set aside an order blocking Input Tax Credit (ITC) for a taxpayer, ruling that the tax authority acted solely on a recommendation without independent evaluation. The court held that such action failed to meet the statutory requirement of recording 'reason to believe.' The decision underscores the need for proper justification in tax enforcement actions.

It ruled that such action does not meet the statutory requirement of reason to believe. articles.title: Souharda Society Wins 80P Deduction – ITAT Rejects Technical Denial & Expands Benefit
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled in favor of Souharda Society, allowing it to claim a Section 80P tax deduction. The decision expands the benefit of Section 80P to similar societies.

articles.title: GST General Penalty Invalid Due to Prior Levy of Section 47 Late Fee: Madras HC
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that imposing both a late fee under Section 47 and a general penalty for the same GST default is unjustified. The Court held that overlapping penalties are unsustainable and clarified that only one penal consequence can apply in such cases. This decision impacts tax compliance and enforcement under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined the legality of imposing both late fee and general penalty for the same default. The Court ruled that such overlapping penalties are unjustified and unsustainable. The decision clarifies that only one penal consequence can be applied in such cases. articles.summary: Russia aims to develop the Northern Sea Route as a key global trade artery between Europe and Asia. articles.title: Jharkhand HC Dismissed Writ Petition Due to Availability of Alternate GST Appeal Remedy
articles.summary: The Jharkhand High Court dismissed a writ petition filed by local self-government bodies seeking GST exemptions, stating that such claims must go through the statutory appellate mechanism rather than bypassing it. The court clarified that not all activities by these bodies are automatically exempt from GST and require factual scrutiny. The ruling underscores the need to follow prescribed legal remedies before approaching courts for tax-related disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Court clarified that local self-government bodies are not automatically exempt from GST on all activities. It ruled that such claims require factual scrutiny and cannot bypass the statutory appellate mechanism. The post Jharkhand HC Dismissed Writ Petition Due to Availability of Alternate GST Appeal Remedy appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has issued a revised Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) to streamline Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) approvals in India. articles.title: LTCG Addition Deleted as Assessee Proved Genuine Investment Activity Through Evidence
articles.summary: An Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a taxpayer's share transactions were genuine based on consistent investment history and documentary proof. The tribunal deleted additions made under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act due to lack of contrary evidence. The ITAT imposed a token cost of ₹1,000 and remanded the matter to the Assessing Officer (AO) for fresh adjudication, ensuring the taxpayer gets a full hearing. The tribunal held that denial of hearing violated natural justice principles.

articles.title: Unlisted Bonds Lose LTCG Benefit; No Capital Gains Exemption for Depreciable Assets under Income Tax Act 2025
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act 2025 removes long-term capital gains (LTCG) benefits for unlisted bonds and eliminates exemptions for gains from depreciable assets. Previously, judicial interpretations allowed some exemptions, but the new law treats all such gains as short-term capital gains. This change affects taxpayers holding depreciable assets or unlisted bonds, removing prior tax benefits.
articles.raw_summary: The new law treats gains from depreciable assets as short-term capital gains for all purposes, not merely for computation. This effectively removes exemption benefits previously allowed through judicial interpretation under the old regime. The post Unlisted Bonds Lose LTCG Benefit; No Capital Gains Exemption for Depreciable Assets under Income Tax Act 2025 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Delhi High Court Permits 100% Visually Impaired Woman To Live With Partner Despite Father's Objection, Says Adult Free To Decide Residence
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has permitted a 100% visually impaired adult woman to reside with her partner of choice, reiterating that a major is free to decide her residence, irrespective of parental objections. A division bench of Justices Navin Chawla and Ravinder Dudeja was hearing a habeas corpus petition filed by the woman's partner, who alleged that she was being confined by her parents against her wishes. Pursuant to the Court's earlier directions, the woman appeared before the bench in person.

articles.title: RBI releases norms for banks holding non financial assets
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has released new draft rules. Banks can now hold specific non-financial assets only to recover bad loans. These assets must be sold within seven years. Selling them back to borrowers is prohibited. This aims for transparency and better recovery. The rules are open for public comment until May 26.

articles.title: US Stock Market | Bond markets signal rising odds of Fed rate hike before cuts
articles.summary: Global bond traders are increasingly betting on a possible US Federal Reserve interest rate hike before any cuts, with over 50% probability by April. This shift reflects rising policy uncertainty, increased hedging, and leadership transition risks, as Kevin Warsh is expected to take charge amid pressure from Donald Trump for lower rates. The move could influence global financial markets, including India's bond and stock markets, due to the Fed's role in setting global liquidity conditions.

The change reflects rising policy uncertainty, increased hedging activity, and leadership transition risks, with Kevin Warsh expected to take charge amid pressure from Donald Trump for lower rates.

articles.title: Excess Stock Found in GST Survey Cannot Trigger Confiscation Proceedings: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that excess stock found during a GST survey cannot trigger confiscation proceedings. The court quashed an order initiating penalty proceedings, stating such cases must be handled under assessment provisions instead. The ruling clarifies the legal framework for GST enforcement and tax administration in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that initiating proceedings under the penalty provision for alleged excess stock discovered during a survey was improper. It ruled that such cases must be dealt with under assessment provisions, leading to quashing of the order. articles.title: Teen rape survivor’s choice vs unborn baby’s right to life—how SC’s new order deviates from precedent
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, led by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, rejected AIIMS' plea to deny a 15-year-old rape survivor the right to terminate her 27-week pregnancy. Earlier court rulings often blocked terminations if the fetus was healthy, but this order deviates from precedent. The decision highlights the conflict between a survivor's choice and the unborn baby's right to life. The case underscores the need for clearer legal frameworks on abortion rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: SC bench led by CJI Surya Kant rejected AIIMS plea against allowing a 15-year-old girl to terminate her 27-week pregnancy. Earlier rulings often denied termination if foetus was in good health.

articles.title: Section 130 Cannot Be Used for Excess Stock Detected in GST Survey: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that Section 130 of the GST law cannot be used to address excess stock detected during a GST survey. The court clarified that the correct legal route for such discrepancies is through Sections 73 or 74 of the GST Act. This ruling reinforces established legal precedents and has implications for how tax authorities handle stock-related discrepancies in GST surveys.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether Section 130 could be used when discrepancies were found during a GST survey. It held that the correct legal route is Sections 73/74 and reinforced settled precedents. articles.title: Recycled Activewear Reveals the Structural Limits of Fashion’s Sustainability Claims
articles.summary: The article examines the sustainability claims of recycled polyester in activewear, noting that most material still comes from PET drink bottles rather than discarded textiles. This raises concerns about whether fashion brands are truly addressing waste or merely shifting it to other recycling systems. The issue has implications for global textile waste management and could influence future environmental and trade policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Recycled polyester has become a default sustainability claim across activewear, swimwear and outerwear, but its supply chain is less circular than its labelling suggests. articles.title: Bail must be considered if right to speedy trial is violated, says SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that bail must be considered if a trial is delayed, as such delays violate the fundamental right to a speedy trial, which is part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that prolonged trials infringe upon these constitutional rights. This ruling sets a legal precedent for future cases involving trial delays.
articles.raw_summary: Delay in trial infringes upon the right to life and personal liberty, noted the court.

Vualnam of India's Finance Ministry warned of 'very difficult' days ahead due to multiple economic stress points and limited visibility following the West Asia crisis. He stressed that fiscal discipline has strengthened the government's position and reiterated that capital expenditure will remain a priority despite rising pressures.
articles.raw_summary: In the first official response after the West Asia crisis, Expenditure Secretary V. Vualnam warned that the outlook is “very difficult” with multiple stress points and limited visibility, even as he said fiscal discipline has put the government on a strong footing and reiterated that capital expenditure will remain a priority despite rising pressures.

articles.title: State-run energy firms can shift over ₹2 trillion annually to clean energy: Report
articles.summary: In FY25, nine state-run energy companies invested ₹2.6 trillion, with ₹2.3 trillion going to fossil fuels and only ₹0.3 trillion to clean energy. The report suggests these firms could shift over ₹2 trillion annually toward clean energy. The data highlights the current dominance of fossil fuel investments and the potential for policy-driven shifts toward renewable energy sources.
articles.raw_summary: In FY25, the nine PSUs together invested ₹2.6 trillion across fossil fuels and clean energy, of this, ₹2.3 trillion was directed towards fossil fuels, compared to about ₹0.3 trillion in clean energy.

articles.title: Sugarcane FRP hiked to Rs 365/quintal for 2026-27 season
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane to ₹365 per quintal for the 2026-27 season. Farmers will receive an additional ₹3.56 per quintal for every 0.1% increase in sugar recovery above this level, with reductions for lower recovery. This decision impacts sugarcane farmers and the sugar industry, aligning pricing with productivity incentives.
articles.raw_summary: Farmers will receive a premium of ₹3.56 per quintal for every 0.1% increase in recovery above this level, with a corresponding reduction for lower recovery.

articles.title: Services, retail lending drives banks’ credit growth to nearly 16% in FY26
articles.summary: India's banking sector saw credit growth of nearly 16% in FY26, driven by strong retail and services lending. Personal loans grew 16.2%, supported by vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit remained stable. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) may monitor these trends for potential policy adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Retail lending remained strong, with personal loans rising 16.2%, up from 11.7% a year earlier, supported by demand for vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit growth remained steady. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit.

The decision impacts airlines, fuel consumers, and industrial sectors, reflecting broader energy pricing dynamics in India.

articles.title: Southeast Asia needs to expand semiconductor production, global trade group SEMI says
articles.summary: A global trade group, SEMI, has urged Southeast Asian countries to increase semiconductor manufacturing by building more fabrication plants over the next decade. The move aims to diversify production away from China and Taiwan, reducing supply chain risks. Currently, most new semiconductor plants are concentrated in these regions, making geographical diversity crucial for industry stability. Southeast Asia must play a larger role in global chip production to ensure supply chain resilience.
articles.raw_summary: Southeast Asian nations are urged to boost semiconductor manufacturing. The region needs more fabrication plants in the next ten years. This will help diversify production and lower supply chain risks. Currently, most new plants are in China and Taiwan. Increased geographical diversity is crucial for industry stability. Southeast Asia must step up its role in global chip production.

articles.title: BJP gains in Bengal may accelerate reforms push amid economic headwinds
articles.summary: The dominant National Democratic Alliance constituent at the Centre has registered its maiden victory in West Bengal, retained Assam and Puducherry (with alliance partners) and won a record three seats in Kerala. It won only one seat in Tamil Nadu, against four in 2021.

articles.title: PMI rises to 54.7; Inflation pressures mount
articles.summary: India's manufacturing sector saw modest growth in April. New orders and production improved, boosting activity. However, rising costs due to the West Asia conflict are a concern. Input prices increased at their fastest pace since August 2022. Output prices also saw a quick rise. Export performance was strong. Hiring activity strengthened, indicating optimism for future expansion.

Hiring activity strengthened, indicating optimism for future expansion.

articles.title: India palm oil imports fall to one-year low in April on weak demand
articles.summary: India's palm oil imports plummeted 27% in April to a one-year low, driven by sluggish institutional demand and a reduced price advantage over rival oils. This decline could impact global palm oil futures, while imports of soyoil and sunflower oil saw significant increases, pushing overall edible oil imports to a 2026 high.

articles.summary: India's manufacturing growth saw a slight uptick in April, reaching 54.7, but remained near a four-year low due to weak domestic demand and rising input costs. Despite challenges, export orders surged, and companies expanded hiring and expressed optimism for future growth.

articles.title: Nicobar is too important to get wrong
articles.summary: This article by Debika Dutta highlights the critical significance of the Nicobar Islands for India's strategic and ecological interests. It emphasizes the need for careful policy decisions to protect the region's unique biodiversity and geopolitical importance. The author argues that mismanagement could have long-term consequences for India's national security and environmental sustainability.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Debika Dutta, columnist and teacher, Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Mangaldai, Assam.

articles.title: Net-zero was never designed for a world at war
articles.summary: The article, authored by researchers from TERI, argues that the global push for net-zero emissions was not designed to account for a world at war. It highlights how geopolitical conflicts disrupt energy transitions and climate goals, which could have implications for India's own climate policies and energy security. The authors suggest that current crises may necessitate a rethink of net-zero strategies.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Ankit Rao, research associate and Meeta Lavania, associate director, TERI.

articles.title: From infectious diseases to cervical cancer, vaccination holds the key
articles.summary: This article is authored by Kavita Ayyagari, country director, Girl Effect, India.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Kavita Ayyagari, country director, Girl Effect, India.

articles.title: Genetic testing: Making advanced screening accessible to all
articles.summary: The article, authored by Chandra Ganjoo, Group CEO of Trivitron Healthcare, explores how genetic testing can be made more accessible to the public. It highlights the role of advanced screening in healthcare and suggests ways to improve availability. The piece emphasizes the need for broader adoption of genetic testing in India's healthcare system.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Chandra Ganjoo, group Chief Executive Office, Trivitron Healthcare.

articles.title: From medical expertise to digital health fluency
articles.summary: Dr. Rajendra Pratap Gupta, Chairman of the Academy of Digital Health Sciences, writes about the need for medical professionals to develop digital health fluency. The article highlights the growing importance of integrating digital tools in healthcare delivery and policy. It emphasizes the shift from traditional medical expertise to a more technology-driven approach in India's healthcare system.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Dr Rajendra Pratap Gupta, chairman, Academy of Digital Health Sciences.

articles.title: Woman Alleges Parasailing Mishap In Goa, Says Rope Tightened Around Her Neck
articles.summary: A woman in Goa alleges a parasailing mishap where a rope tightened around her neck, causing injury. She warned other tourists to verify safety measures before engaging in water sports. The incident raises concerns about adventure tourism regulations and consumer protection in India.
articles.raw_summary: The woman also issued a warning to other tourists as she said that before trying any water sport, ask questions and check safety.

articles.title: Govt has eased FDI norms for foreign cos having small Chinese/Hong Kong stake: An explainer
articles.summary: India has eased Foreign Direct Investment rules for companies with up to ten percent Chinese or Hong Kong shareholding. These firms can now invest in India through the automatic route starting May 1, 2026. This change aims to boost investment flows. Previously, stricter rules applied due to security concerns. The government has also expedited clearances for certain manufacturing sectors.
articles.raw_summary: India has eased Foreign Direct Investment rules for companies with up to ten percent Chinese or Hong Kong shareholding. These firms can now invest in India through the automatic route starting May 1, 2026. This change aims to boost investment flows. Previously, stricter rules applied due to security concerns. The government has also expedited clearances for certain manufacturing sectors.
